item.title: Galaxy Tab S9+: Best cases, screen protectors, and accessories in 2025
item.itunes:title: Galaxy Tab S9+: Best cases, screen protectors, and accessories in 2025
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Outside of iPads, Samsung's Galaxy tablets are second to none and easily rank among the best Android tablets you can buy today. While the flagship Galaxy Tab S9 Ultra gets all the praise for being the best, we believe the Galaxy Tab S9+ is equally good, as it brings a lot of noteworthy improvements over the Tab S8 series. That being said, it has a large 12.4-inch display, making it a bit difficult to use with one hand, especially if you're used to a relatively small tablet. We strongly recommend grabbing a case to protect it from accidental drops and scuffs. The best Galaxy Tab S9+ cases can also upgrade how your tablet looks and feels.

But it's not just protective cases you might want for your Android tablet. Samsung hasn't included a charger so you'll want to pick up a 45W model, and a battery pack might not be a bad idea as well. At least you get the S Pen in the box, and the better cases all have a protective area to keep that stylus safe in transit. While the Galaxy Tab S10+ is out now, it's also significantly more expensive, and the Tab S9+ is no slouch, so you can get a good deal by getting the previous model.

 Related
Samsung Galaxy Tab S9 series deals: Save big on the brand's latest flagship tablets

These are some of the best deals you'll find on Samsung's Galaxy Tab S9, Tab S9+, and Tab S9 Ultra.

Best cases

  • Editor's Choice
    Spigen Rugged Armor Pro for Samsung Galaxy Tab S9+

    The Spigen Rugged Armor Pro strikes the ideal balance between protection and portability and is among the best cases you can buy for the Tab S9+ right now. This particular case comes with raised edges to protect the display, an S Pen garage, and a folio cover that doubles as a kickstand. It's also compatible with most brands of screen protectors, which is great.

    $30 at Amazon
  • Best value
    Fintie Hybrid Slim Case for Samsung Galaxy Tab S9+

    The Fintie Hybrid Slim Case is a clear case that comes with a PC back panel and a flexible TPU bumper on the sides for added protection. It also has a folio cover that folds back, allowing you to prop up the tablet on a flat surface for a hands-free experience. Notably, it also has a built-in S Pen groove at the back.

    $15 at Amazon
  • Promoted Pick
    Supcase UB Pro Series Case

    If you want to ensure your Galaxy Tab S9+ stays looking day-one fresh, the Supcase UB Pro is a great rugged case option. With its dual-layer design, built-in screen protector, and raised bumper frame, the UP Pro is built to withstand some serious falls. It also has a rubber port cover to keep dust out of your charging port and a sturdy S Pen holder slot. And if that wasn’t enough features, this case also has a built-in media viewing kickstand.

    $30 at Amazon

Cases with pen holders

  • Best folio case
    JETech Slim Folio stand cover for Galaxy Tab S9+
    $14 $19 Save $5

    The JTech Slim Folio cover is an easy recommendation for those who are looking for a reliable case for their tablet. Not only does this case offer complete 360-degree protection, but it also adds a kickstand with multi-angle viewing support and also a dedicated slot for the S-Pen stylus when in transit.

    $14 at Amazon
  • Rugged kickstand case
    UAG Metropolis SE Black cover for Galaxy Tab S9+
    $68 $80 Save $12

    The rugged Metropolis SE case from Urban Armor Gear provides military-grade protection for the Tab S9+. It's a little on the expensive side, but it's a great rugged case overall that comes with a kickstand, and even an S Pen holder at the back. It's available in black and navy blue colorways, both of which have a multi-angle kickstand and support auto sleep/wake function.

    $68 at Amazon
  • Folio cover with extra pocket
    Fintie Folio Case

    The Fintie Folio Case is relatively simple in its design, but that’s a good thing. After all, keeping your Tab S9+ safe from accidental damage shouldn’t be complicated. Its synthetic leather material is padded enough to absorb light drops and scratches, and its 360-degree design provides you with a safe space to store your S Pen. As an added plus, it has a document pocket in the back for holding small items or papers.

    $24 at Amazon

Keyboard cases

  • Official keyboard case
    Samsung Book Cover Keyboard Slim for Samsung Galaxy Tab S9+

    Samsung's official keyboard case is the one to get if you're looking for a reliable and premium pick for your Galaxy Tab S9+/ S9 FE+. Just like the one that's available for the Galaxy Tab S8+, this particular keyboard case also has responsive buttons and a clean layout. It's also a folio case, meaning it offers 360-degree protection for your tablet.

    $160 at Amazon $160 at Samsung
  • Slim protective folio
    ProCase Keyboard Case

    While it won’t win any beauty pageants, the ProCase Keyboard Case is a very useful accessory for your Tab S9+. Not only does it include a magnetically detachable wireless keyboard, but it also offers a secure slot to store your S Pen and offers total screen protection with its folio cover. And like with all keyboard cases, the folio cover can be folded back for use as a media kickstand.

    $25 at Amazon
  • Floating keyboard case
    Dexnor Samsung Galaxy Tab Cantilever Keyboard Case
    $117 $130 Save $13

    The Dexnor Galaxy Tab case combines an ergonomic keyboard with a floating cantilever design that lets you set your tablet at the perfect angle for use as a 2-in-1 laptop. Its sturdy design is also compatible with a wide range of Galaxy tablets, from the Galaxy Tab S9+ to the Tab S7 series.

    $117 at Amazon

Rugged and waterproof cases

  • Slim rugged case
    Poetic TurtleSkin case for Samsung Galaxy Tab S9+

    The Poetic TurtleSkin Case is a slim yet tough snap-on case that shields your Tab S9+ from drops, scratches, and bumps without adding too much bulk to the overall package. Its large raised bezel protects the vulnerable edges of your tablet, and the rear S Pen holder firmly locks the accessory in place while it charges.

    $23 at Amazon
  • Colorful yet capable
    Miersherk Heavy Duty Rugged Case

    The Miersherk Heavy Duty Rugged Case should be a go-to option for anyone with a history of accidentally damaging their expensive devices. With three distinct layers of drop protection, a built-in screen protector, and a secure S Pen holder, it delivers the whole package. It comes in loads of colors too. Just be warned that this case is bulky.

    $30 at Amazon
  • Maximum utility and protection
    Supfives Rugged Protective Case

    The Supfives Rugged Protective Case is the Swiss Army knife of Tab S9+ cases. It has loads of useful features on top of being an all-around great protective case. These include a swiveling rear kickstand/grip, a secure slot for your S Pen, a detachable shoulder strap, and a bottle opener because why not? It is fairly inexpensive, too so you’re getting great value even if you only ever use half of these features.

    $25 at Amazon

Screen protectors

  • Best tempered glass
    Spigen GlasTR EZ Fit tempered glass for Galaxy Tab S9+
    $27 $45 Save $18

    The Spigen GlasTR EZ Fit is among the best screen protectors you can buy for the Galaxy Tab S9+. This tempered glass protector offers solid protection for your tablet's screen and plays nicely with most cases out there. It also comes with an alignment tool for easier installation.

    $27 at Amazon
  • Matte screen protector
    Junfire Matte Screen Protector 2-Pack

    This two-pack of Junfire screen protectors is yet another viable protective option. It is made of tempered glass and is 0.33mm thick, just like most other options. The standout feature of the Junfire screen protectors, though, is that they sport a matte finish that reduces glare. Just keep in mind that this comes with the tradeoff of letting through less light from the display.

    $14 at Amazon
  • Best privacy screen
    ProCase Privacy Screen Protector
    $17 $25 Save $8

    If you plan to use your Tab S9+ for personal matters, there is no better way to secure your own privacy than this ProCase Privacy Screen Protector. This tempered glass accessory is designed to limit the screen’s functional viewing angle to +/- 30 degrees, ensuring prying eyes cannot easily see what is on your screen. It also ensures that accidental damage doesn’t turn your screen into a spiderweb.

    $17 at Amazon

Chargers and accessories

  • Official Samsung charger
    Samsung 45W USB-C Fast Charger
    $30 $33 Save $3

    Samsung's Galaxy Tab S9+ doesn't come with a charger in the box, but this particular 45W power adapter can solve your charging woes. It supports fast charging and can juice your tablet’s battery back to full in less than a few hours.

    $30 at Amazon
  • Beefy power bank
    Ugreen Nexode 20,000mAh Power Bank
    6 / 10
    $67 $100 Save $33

    The Ugreen Nexode power bank has 20,000 mAh of reserve power to recharge your Galaxy Tab S9+ or anything else you may happen to be carrying. With three outputs and a total of 130W power delivery it'll get your tablet juiced up again in no time.

    $67 at Amazon
  • Best earbuds
    Samsung Galaxy Buds 2 Pro
    $115 $230 Save $115

    The Galaxy Buds 2 Pro are Samsung's premium earbuds offering the best noise-canceling, 3D audio support, and more. Thanks to IPX7 water resistance, these are great for workouts, and with transparency mode, you'll be able to hear what's going on around you — if you want to.

    $115 at Amazon $180 at Best Buy

Your Galaxy Tab S9+ deserves the best accessories

While the Galaxy Tab S9+ isn't as beastly in all respects as the Galaxy Tab S9 Ultra and the new Galaxy Tab S10+ is on shelves now, this 2023 model is still a powerful and versatile tablet. Still, you can unlock even more power and versatility with some choice accessories. Any tablet as nice as the Tab S9+ deserves a protective case at the very least, and the Spigen Rugged Armor Pro is an excellent choice overall.

If you plan to use this tablet for productivity, Samsung's Book Cover Slim Keyboard case is a massive upgrade over the on-screen touch keyboard. If you're looking for a case that can withstand the wear and tear of an active lifestyle, the Supcase UB Pro Series Case offers an even more rugged suite of protective features. Don't forget to throw in a Spigen screen protector for complete coverage.

There are many ways to enhance your tablet-using experience beyond keeping your device safe from harm. You can transform your tablet setup into a true mobile workstation with headphones, speakers, stands, and a more powerful charger. Or, you can just use it to play Subway Surfers. It's all up to you.

 Related
Best tablets in 2025

From iPads to Android and Windows devices, these are the best tablets you can buy in 2024

  • Best value
    Fintie Hybrid Slim Case for Samsung Galaxy Tab S9+

    The Fintie Hybrid Slim Case is a clear case that comes with a PC back panel and a flexible TPU bumper on the sides for added protection. It also has a folio cover that folds back, allowing you to prop up the tablet on a flat surface for a hands-free experience. Notably, it also has a built-in S Pen groove at the back.

    $15 at Amazon
  • Promoted Pick
    Supcase UB Pro Series Case

    If you want to ensure your Galaxy Tab S9+ stays looking day-one fresh, the Supcase UB Pro is a great rugged case option. With its dual-layer design, built-in screen protector, and raised bumper frame, the UP Pro is built to withstand some serious falls. It also has a rubber port cover to keep dust out of your charging port and a sturdy S Pen holder slot. And if that wasn’t enough features, this case also has a built-in media viewing kickstand.

    $30 at Amazon

Cases with pen holders

  • Best folio case
    JETech Slim Folio stand cover for Galaxy Tab S9+
    $14 $19 Save $5

    The JTech Slim Folio cover is an easy recommendation for those who are looking for a reliable case for their tablet. Not only does this case offer complete 360-degree protection, but it also adds a kickstand with multi-angle viewing support and also a dedicated slot for the S-Pen stylus when in transit.

    $14 at Amazon
  • Rugged kickstand case
    UAG Metropolis SE Black cover for Galaxy Tab S9+
    $68 $80 Save $12

    The rugged Metropolis SE case from Urban Armor Gear provides military-grade protection for the Tab S9+. It's a little on the expensive side, but it's a great rugged case overall that comes with a kickstand, and even an S Pen holder at the back. It's available in black and navy blue colorways, both of which have a multi-angle kickstand and support auto sleep/wake function.

    $68 at Amazon
  • Folio cover with extra pocket
    Fintie Folio Case

    The Fintie Folio Case is relatively simple in its design, but that’s a good thing. After all, keeping your Tab S9+ safe from accidental damage shouldn’t be complicated. Its synthetic leather material is padded enough to absorb light drops and scratches, and its 360-degree design provides you with a safe space to store your S Pen. As an added plus, it has a document pocket in the back for holding small items or papers.

    $24 at Amazon

Keyboard cases

  • Official keyboard case
    Samsung Book Cover Keyboard Slim for Samsung Galaxy Tab S9+

    Samsung's official keyboard case is the one to get if you're looking for a reliable and premium pick for your Galaxy Tab S9+/ S9 FE+. Just like the one that's available for the Galaxy Tab S8+, this particular keyboard case also has responsive buttons and a clean layout. It's also a folio case, meaning it offers 360-degree protection for your tablet.

    $160 at Amazon $160 at Samsung
  • Slim protective folio
    ProCase Keyboard Case

    While it won’t win any beauty pageants, the ProCase Keyboard Case is a very useful accessory for your Tab S9+. Not only does it include a magnetically detachable wireless keyboard, but it also offers a secure slot to store your S Pen and offers total screen protection with its folio cover. And like with all keyboard cases, the folio cover can be folded back for use as a media kickstand.

    $25 at Amazon
  • Floating keyboard case
    Dexnor Samsung Galaxy Tab Cantilever Keyboard Case
    $117 $130 Save $13

    The Dexnor Galaxy Tab case combines an ergonomic keyboard with a floating cantilever design that lets you set your tablet at the perfect angle for use as a 2-in-1 laptop. Its sturdy design is also compatible with a wide range of Galaxy tablets, from the Galaxy Tab S9+ to the Tab S7 series.

    $117 at Amazon

Rugged and waterproof cases

  • Slim rugged case
    Poetic TurtleSkin case for Samsung Galaxy Tab S9+

    The Poetic TurtleSkin Case is a slim yet tough snap-on case that shields your Tab S9+ from drops, scratches, and bumps without adding too much bulk to the overall package. Its large raised bezel protects the vulnerable edges of your tablet, and the rear S Pen holder firmly locks the accessory in place while it charges.

    $23 at Amazon
  • Colorful yet capable
    Miersherk Heavy Duty Rugged Case

    The Miersherk Heavy Duty Rugged Case should be a go-to option for anyone with a history of accidentally damaging their expensive devices. With three distinct layers of drop protection, a built-in screen protector, and a secure S Pen holder, it delivers the whole package. It comes in loads of colors too. Just be warned that this case is bulky.

    $30 at Amazon
  • Maximum utility and protection
    Supfives Rugged Protective Case

    The Supfives Rugged Protective Case is the Swiss Army knife of Tab S9+ cases. It has loads of useful features on top of being an all-around great protective case. These include a swiveling rear kickstand/grip, a secure slot for your S Pen, a detachable shoulder strap, and a bottle opener because why not? It is fairly inexpensive, too so you’re getting great value even if you only ever use half of these features.

    $25 at Amazon

Screen protectors

  • Best tempered glass
    Spigen GlasTR EZ Fit tempered glass for Galaxy Tab S9+
    $27 $45 Save $18

    The Spigen GlasTR EZ Fit is among the best screen protectors you can buy for the Galaxy Tab S9+. This tempered glass protector offers solid protection for your tablet's screen and plays nicely with most cases out there. It also comes with an alignment tool for easier installation.

    $27 at Amazon
  • Matte screen protector
    Junfire Matte Screen Protector 2-Pack

    This two-pack of Junfire screen protectors is yet another viable protective option. It is made of tempered glass and is 0.33mm thick, just like most other options. The standout feature of the Junfire screen protectors, though, is that they sport a matte finish that reduces glare. Just keep in mind that this comes with the tradeoff of letting through less light from the display.

    $14 at Amazon
  • Best privacy screen
    ProCase Privacy Screen Protector
    $17 $25 Save $8

    If you plan to use your Tab S9+ for personal matters, there is no better way to secure your own privacy than this ProCase Privacy Screen Protector. This tempered glass accessory is designed to limit the screen’s functional viewing angle to +/- 30 degrees, ensuring prying eyes cannot easily see what is on your screen. It also ensures that accidental damage doesn’t turn your screen into a spiderweb.

    $17 at Amazon

Chargers and accessories

  • Official Samsung charger
    Samsung 45W USB-C Fast Charger
    $30 $33 Save $3

    Samsung's Galaxy Tab S9+ doesn't come with a charger in the box, but this particular 45W power adapter can solve your charging woes. It supports fast charging and can juice your tablet’s battery back to full in less than a few hours.

    $30 at Amazon
  • Beefy power bank
    Ugreen Nexode 20,000mAh Power Bank
    6 / 10
    $67 $100 Save $33

    The Ugreen Nexode power bank has 20,000 mAh of reserve power to recharge your Galaxy Tab S9+ or anything else you may happen to be carrying. With three outputs and a total of 130W power delivery it'll get your tablet juiced up again in no time.

    $67 at Amazon
  • Best earbuds
    Samsung Galaxy Buds 2 Pro
    $115 $230 Save $115

    The Galaxy Buds 2 Pro are Samsung's premium earbuds offering the best noise-canceling, 3D audio support, and more. Thanks to IPX7 water resistance, these are great for workouts, and with transparency mode, you'll be able to hear what's going on around you — if you want to.

    $115 at Amazon $180 at Best Buy

Your Galaxy Tab S9+ deserves the best accessories

While the Galaxy Tab S9+ isn't as beastly in all respects as the Galaxy Tab S9 Ultra and the new Galaxy Tab S10+ is on shelves now, this 2023 model is still a powerful and versatile tablet. Still, you can unlock even more power and versatility with some choice accessories. Any tablet as nice as the Tab S9+ deserves a protective case at the very least, and the Spigen Rugged Armor Pro is an excellent choice overall.

If you plan to use this tablet for productivity, Samsung's Book Cover Slim Keyboard case is a massive upgrade over the on-screen touch keyboard. If you're looking for a case that can withstand the wear and tear of an active lifestyle, the Supcase UB Pro Series Case offers an even more rugged suite of protective features. Don't forget to throw in a Spigen screen protector for complete coverage.

There are many ways to enhance your tablet-using experience beyond keeping your device safe from harm. You can transform your tablet setup into a true mobile workstation with headphones, speakers, stands, and a more powerful charger. Or, you can just use it to play Subway Surfers. It's all up to you.

 Related
Best tablets in 2025

From iPads to Android and Windows devices, these are the best tablets you can buy in 2024
item.content:encoded:
Outside of iPads, Samsung's Galaxy tablets are second to none and easily rank among the best Android tablets you can buy today. While the flagship Galaxy Tab S9 Ultra gets all the praise for being the best, we believe the Galaxy Tab S9+ is equally good, as it brings a lot of noteworthy improvements over the Tab S8 series. That being said, it has a large 12.4-inch display, making it a bit difficult to use with one hand, especially if you're used to a relatively small tablet. We strongly recommend grabbing a case to protect it from accidental drops and scuffs. The best Galaxy Tab S9+ cases can also upgrade how your tablet looks and feels.

But it's not just protective cases you might want for your Android tablet. Samsung hasn't included a charger so you'll want to pick up a 45W model, and a battery pack might not be a bad idea as well. At least you get the S Pen in the box, and the better cases all have a protective area to keep that stylus safe in transit. While the Galaxy Tab S10+ is out now, it's also significantly more expensive, and the Tab S9+ is no slouch, so you can get a good deal by getting the previous model.

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy S23+ cases: 12 picks that we love
item.itunes:title: Best Samsung Galaxy S23+ cases: 12 picks that we love
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Samsung's Galaxy S23 series devices may be a couple of years old now, but they're still fantastic phones, and if you have one, there's no reason to throw it away just yet. These devices still have great specs and will continue to receive updates for the couple of years, so you can save some money and avoid buying a new phone. All three phones are equally good, but the Galaxy S23+, in particular, is great for those who want a bigger display without splurging on the Ultra model.

It's a fantastic device featuring one of Qualcomm's flagship chipsets, a nice set of cameras, a gorgeous AMOLED display, and a large battery with 45W fast charging support. Along with these hardware improvements, the Galaxy S23+ comes with Corning's Gorilla Glass Victus 2 for improved drop protection. That said, we recommend using a case with it to ensure it remains protected from scratches and cracks. If you're wondering which one to buy, we've highlighted some good options below.

  • Editor's choice
    Crave Dual Guard for Samsung Galaxy S23+

    For less than 20 bucks, the Crave Dual Guard has just about everything you'd want in a case for your Samsung Galaxy S23+. It provides sturdy dual-layer protection for your phone with an external hard shell surrounding a soft shockproof inner layer. It also has a nice texture that feels great in the hand and is available in a variety of great-looking colors.

    $18 at Amazon
  • Best value
    Speck Presidio 2 Grip Samsung Galaxy S23+ Case
    $6 $40 Save $34

    The Speck Presidio 2 Grip is an affordable no-frills case for the Samsung Galaxy S23+. It offers great drop protection with its Armor Cloud layer that absorbs hard shocks, yet retains a slim, pocket-friendly design that's compatible with wireless chargers. Plus, the grippy texture helps prevent accidental drops.

    $6 at Amazon
  • Promoted Pick
    Supcase UB Pro for Galaxy S23+

    The Supcase Unicorn Beetle Pro is a complete rugged protefction solution for the Samsung Galaxy S23+. It includes a tough case that doubles as a kickstand and even comes with a belt clip, adding ore functionaliuty to the phone without extra accessories.

    $25 at Amazon
  • Premium pick
    dbrand Grip for Galaxy S23+

    dbrand's Grip is a premium protective case that has a unique selling point: endless customization. You can personalize it with dbrand's extensive lineup of skins and give your phone a unique look while keeping it safe from scratches and drops. Skip buying a new case every few months with dbrand's Grip case for the Galaxy S23+.

    $50 at dbrand
  • Best clear case
    Spigen Ultra Hybrid for Galaxy S23+
    $17 $35 Save $18

    If you want to protect your Galaxy S23+ without hiding its natural beauty, the Spigen Ultra Hybrid clear case is for you. It offers slim, yet sturdy protection with reinforced corners, and it lets the colors of your phone shine through perfectly.

    $17 at Amazon
  • Best thin case
    TORRAS Shockproof Case for Galaxy S23+

    This is a good option to consider for those who don't want to add too much bulk to their phone in the name of protecting it. The Torras Shockproof case, despite its thin form-factor, offers solid protection to keep secure your device from accidental drops and scuffs.

    $20 at Amazon
  • Premium fabric case
    Peak Design Everyday Case for Galaxy S23+
    $32 $40 Save $8

    The Peak Design Everyday Case is another great premium option for your new phone. It's a slim yet durable case with a fabric panel that gives it a comfortable grip. It also supports Peak Design's extensive ecosystem of accessories, like various mounts, tripod, detachable wallet, wireless charger, and more.

    $32 at Peak Design
  • Best rugged case
    OtterBox Defender case for Samsung Galaxy S23+
    $19 $28 Save $9

    The OtterBox Defender is one of the most rugged cases out there for the Galaxy S23+. Not only does it have raised edges on the front and back to protect the display and camera sensors, but it also offers port protection. It comes in a few different colors, all of which offer microbial defense protection too.

    $19 at Amazon
  • Built-in screen protector
    Poetic Revolution for Galaxy S23+

    The Poetic Revolution case is designed for all-around protection because not only is it a case, but it also includes a screen protector that goes with it, keeping the front and back of your phone safe. Plus, it doubles as a kickstand, making it easy to stand up the phone.

    $20 at Amazon
  • Best leather case
    Bellroy Leather Case for Galaxy S23+

    There's nothing quite like the feeling of leather in your hand, and if that's something you're interested in, then the Bellroy Leather Case is for you. The soft touch leather on the back of this case gives your phone a premium feel that ages beautifully on top of protecting it.

    $29 at Amazon
  • Premium leather folio
    Shieldon Leather Folio for Galaxy S23+

    Get this leather folio case from Shieldon for your Galaxy S23+ and ditch your old-school wallet for good. It offers all-around protection for your device and features built-in card slots that can house up to three cards. You also get an RFID block layer and a dedicated slot for carrying some cash.

    $31 at Amazon
  • Built-in kickstand
    ESR Boost Kickstand for Galaxy S23+
    $17 $20 Save $3

    If you like clear cases but don't want to settle with something as basic as the Spigen Ultra Hybrid, the ESR Boost Kickstand is a great alternative. It's a premium clear case that offers complete coverage for your Galaxy S23+ and a built-in kickstand that lets you prop your phone up for ease of use.

    $17 at Amazon

There's plenty of cases for the Samsung Gaaxy S23+

That rounds up our collection of the best Galaxy S23+ cases on the market. The Crave Dual Guard case is our favorite out of the lot, as it's one of the most stylish cases on the list and gives the phone a unique fit and finish. The Torras Shockproof case is also good, but remember that it's pretty thin and not nearly as protective as other options highlighted on this list. If you don't mind bulky cases, the Supcase UB Pro and the dbrand Grip case are great alternatives with ample cushioning to protect your phone.

If you're considering investing in additional accessories, opt for the Peak Design Everyday Case. It's compatible with a wide range of Peak Design accessories, like car and phone mounts, wireless chargers, tripods, detachable wallets, and more. If you've already found a case that fits your needs, head to our roundup of the best Galaxy S23+ screen protectors to add an extra layer of protection to the screen.

 Related
Samsung Galaxy S23+ review: Mind the competition

The Galaxy S23+ is an excellent flagship smartphone with long battery life and fast performance, but competition is fierce at its $999 price point.

It offers all-around protection for your device and features built-in card slots that can house up to three cards. You also get an RFID block layer and a dedicated slot for carrying some cash.

    $31 at Amazon
  • Built-in kickstand
    ESR Boost Kickstand for Galaxy S23+
    $17 $20 Save $3

    If you like clear cases but don't want to settle with something as basic as the Spigen Ultra Hybrid, the ESR Boost Kickstand is a great alternative. It's a premium clear case that offers complete coverage for your Galaxy S23+ and a built-in kickstand that lets you prop your phone up for ease of use.

    $17 at Amazon

There's plenty of cases for the Samsung Gaaxy S23+

That rounds up our collection of the best Galaxy S23+ cases on the market. The Crave Dual Guard case is our favorite out of the lot, as it's one of the most stylish cases on the list and gives the phone a unique fit and finish. The Torras Shockproof case is also good, but remember that it's pretty thin and not nearly as protective as other options highlighted on this list. If you don't mind bulky cases, the Supcase UB Pro and the dbrand Grip case are great alternatives with ample cushioning to protect your phone.

If you're considering investing in additional accessories, opt for the Peak Design Everyday Case. It's compatible with a wide range of Peak Design accessories, like car and phone mounts, wireless chargers, tripods, detachable wallets, and more. If you've already found a case that fits your needs, head to our roundup of the best Galaxy S23+ screen protectors to add an extra layer of protection to the screen.

 Related
Samsung Galaxy S23+ review: Mind the competition

The Galaxy S23+ is an excellent flagship smartphone with long battery life and fast performance, but competition is fierce at its $999 price point.
item.content:encoded:
Samsung's Galaxy S23 series devices may be a couple of years old now, but they're still fantastic phones, and if you have one, there's no reason to throw it away just yet. These devices still have great specs and will continue to receive updates for the couple of years, so you can save some money and avoid buying a new phone. All three phones are equally good, but the Galaxy S23+, in particular, is great for those who want a bigger display without splurging on the Ultra model.

It's a fantastic device featuring one of Qualcomm's flagship chipsets, a nice set of cameras, a gorgeous AMOLED display, and a large battery with 45W fast charging support. Along with these hardware improvements, the Galaxy S23+ comes with Corning's Gorilla Glass Victus 2 for improved drop protection. That said, we recommend using a case with it to ensure it remains protected from scratches and cracks. If you're wondering which one to buy, we've highlighted some good options below.

  • Editor's choice
    Crave Dual Guard for Samsung Galaxy S23+

    For less than 20 bucks, the Crave Dual Guard has just about everything you'd want in a case for your Samsung Galaxy S23+. It provides sturdy dual-layer protection for your phone with an external hard shell surrounding a soft shockproof inner layer. It also has a nice texture that feels great in the hand and is available in a variety of great-looking colors.

    $18 at Amazon
  • Best value
    Speck Presidio 2 Grip Samsung Galaxy S23+ Case
    $6 $40 Save $34

    The Speck Presidio 2 Grip is an affordable no-frills case for the Samsung Galaxy S23+. It offers great drop protection with its Armor Cloud layer that absorbs hard shocks, yet retains a slim, pocket-friendly design that's compatible with wireless chargers. Plus, the grippy texture helps prevent accidental drops.

    $6 at Amazon
  • Promoted Pick
    Supcase UB Pro for Galaxy S23+

    The Supcase Unicorn Beetle Pro is a complete rugged protefction solution for the Samsung Galaxy S23+. It includes a tough case that doubles as a kickstand and even comes with a belt clip, adding ore functionaliuty to the phone without extra accessories.

    $25 at Amazon
  • Premium pick
    dbrand Grip for Galaxy S23+

    dbrand's Grip is a premium protective case that has a unique selling point: endless customization. You can personalize it with dbrand's extensive lineup of skins and give your phone a unique look while keeping it safe from scratches and drops. Skip buying a new case every few months with dbrand's Grip case for the Galaxy S23+.

    $50 at dbrand
  • Best clear case
    Spigen Ultra Hybrid for Galaxy S23+
    $17 $35 Save $18

    If you want to protect your Galaxy S23+ without hiding its natural beauty, the Spigen Ultra Hybrid clear case is for you. It offers slim, yet sturdy protection with reinforced corners, and it lets the colors of your phone shine through perfectly.

    $17 at Amazon
  • Best thin case
    TORRAS Shockproof Case for Galaxy S23+

    This is a good option to consider for those who don't want to add too much bulk to their phone in the name of protecting it. The Torras Shockproof case, despite its thin form-factor, offers solid protection to keep secure your device from accidental drops and scuffs.

    $20 at Amazon
  • Premium fabric case
    Peak Design Everyday Case for Galaxy S23+
    $32 $40 Save $8

    The Peak Design Everyday Case is another great premium option for your new phone. It's a slim yet durable case with a fabric panel that gives it a comfortable grip. It also supports Peak Design's extensive ecosystem of accessories, like various mounts, tripod, detachable wallet, wireless charger, and more.

    $32 at Peak Design
  • Best rugged case
    OtterBox Defender case for Samsung Galaxy S23+
    $19 $28 Save $9

    The OtterBox Defender is one of the most rugged cases out there for the Galaxy S23+. Not only does it have raised edges on the front and back to protect the display and camera sensors, but it also offers port protection. It comes in a few different colors, all of which offer microbial defense protection too.

    $19 at Amazon
  • Built-in screen protector
    Poetic Revolution for Galaxy S23+

    The Poetic Revolution case is designed for all-around protection because not only is it a case, but it also includes a screen protector that goes with it, keeping the front and back of your phone safe. Plus, it doubles as a kickstand, making it easy to stand up the phone.

    $20 at Amazon
  • Best leather case
    Bellroy Leather Case for Galaxy S23+

    There's nothing quite like the feeling of leather in your hand, and if that's something you're interested in, then the Bellroy Leather Case is for you. The soft touch leather on the back of this case gives your phone a premium feel that ages beautifully on top of protecting it.

    $29 at Amazon
  • Premium leather folio
    Shieldon Leather Folio for Galaxy S23+

    Get this leather folio case from Shieldon for your Galaxy S23+ and ditch your old-school wallet for good. It offers all-around protection for your device and features built-in card slots that can house up to three cards. You also get an RFID block layer and a dedicated slot for carrying some cash.

    $31 at Amazon
  • Built-in kickstand
    ESR Boost Kickstand for Galaxy S23+
    $17 $20 Save $3

    If you like clear cases but don't want to settle with something as basic as the Spigen Ultra Hybrid, the ESR Boost Kickstand is a great alternative. It's a premium clear case that offers complete coverage for your Galaxy S23+ and a built-in kickstand that lets you prop your phone up for ease of use.

    $17 at Amazon

There's plenty of cases for the Samsung Gaaxy S23+

That rounds up our collection of the best Galaxy S23+ cases on the market. The Crave Dual Guard case is our favorite out of the lot, as it's one of the most stylish cases on the list and gives the phone a unique fit and finish. The Torras Shockproof case is also good, but remember that it's pretty thin and not nearly as protective as other options highlighted on this list. If you don't mind bulky cases, the Supcase UB Pro and the dbrand Grip case are great alternatives with ample cushioning to protect your phone.

If you're considering investing in additional accessories, opt for the Peak Design Everyday Case. It's compatible with a wide range of Peak Design accessories, like car and phone mounts, wireless chargers, tripods, detachable wallets, and more. If you've already found a case that fits your needs, head to our roundup of the best Galaxy S23+ screen protectors to add an extra layer of protection to the screen.

item.title: The wait is over, Microsoft Teams users finally get the long-awaited notification freedom they wished for
item.itunes:title: The wait is over, Microsoft Teams users finally get the long-awaited notification freedom they wished for
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Summary

  • Microsoft Teams now lets you customize banner notification positions for better focus and productivity.
  • Users had long requested this change, leading to over 20,000 views and 387 similar questions on the Community website.
  • The new notification feature is currently exclusive to Teams Public Preview or Microsoft 365 Targeted release on Teams Windows client.

Microsoft has some high-impact features planned for Teams users this year. In addition, the software giant also listens to users' feedback to address the pain points and improve productivity applications and services. We've recently seen this in action when Microsoft finally decided to add true dark mode to Excel sheets. Add one more to the list, as Microsoft has finally announced a much-requested notification feature for Teams.

Microsoft Teams now allows you to customize the position of banner notifications

Image: Microsoft

In a Techcommunity blog post, Microsoft has announced that Teams will now allow users to change the position of banner notifications for "greater focus," and "improved productivity." There are four options to choose from: Bottom right, Top right, Bottom left, and Top left. To select whatever best works for you, you need to go to Settings > Notifications and activity > Display and choose the position you want them to appear in.

Teams users have long been requesting Microsoft to "fix" the notification banner position. In a Microsoft Community post, one user complained about notification banners appearing slightly above the bottom right corner. At the time of writing, 387 people also had the same issue, and as many as over 20,000 people viewed the post on the Community website, indicating that many Teams users wanted Microsoft to change the position of notification banners.

Not only did Microsoft push the notification banner a little to the bottom of the right, but it also gave users more freedom to choose the location of banner notifications. However, these notification control options are not available for everyone at this point. It's exclusive to members of the Teams Public Preview or Microsoft 365 Targeted release on Teams Windows client. If you're using it and have ideas to further improve Teams notifications, Microsoft encourages you to send feedback by going to Settings and more > Feedback in the top right corner.

 Related
Here are all the new features Microsoft added to Teams in December 2024

Although the change log for Microsoft Teams is quite brief this time, it contains a major upgrade for users of the web client.

item.title: You can now 3D print the "Pi Box" that lets you bring a Raspberry Pi 4 wherever you please
item.itunes:title: You can now 3D print the "Pi Box" that lets you bring a Raspberry Pi 4 wherever you please
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Summary

  • Pi Box is portable with Raspberry Pi 4 and display powered by a battery pack circuit.
  • Features a 3D-printed shell with compartments for the battery, Pi 4, and screen.
  • Instructions for building your own Pi Box are available on the Hackaday page.

Raspberry Pis are small enough to bring everywhere you go, but how about using them at the same time? While you can grab a Pi laptop or cyberdeck, they can be a little pricey. Fortunately, the Pi Box is here to keep things simple, offering a 3D-printed shell and a screen so you can use your Raspberry Pi 4 anywhere you like.

 Related
Elecrow CrowView Note 14 review: a budget-friendly way to turn your Raspberry Pi into a laptop

This all-in-one display, keyboard, and trackpad is an easy way to turn any computing device into a laptop

1

The Pi Box is a portable way to bring your SBC wherever you go

As posted by Arnov Sharma on Hackaday.io, the Pi Box is designed to be a self-contained box that lets you use your Pi while on the move. It combines the Raspberry Pi 4, a display, a battery, and a 3D-printed shell:

Here, we are using Rasperry Pi 4 combined with the DFRobot's 5'' 800x480 IPS display, and both are being powered by a battery pack circuit we created for a previous project. We are using a 11.1V lithium cell battery pack. The Pi 4 and Display need stable 5V to operate so to step down the 11.1V from the battery pack, we prepared a custom Buck converter circuit that provides stable 5V 3A to power the Pi.

The case itself is comprised of two compartments. The back one holds the battery, while the front one houses the Raspberry Pi 4 and the screen. Plus, the enclosure has its very own handle, so you can lug it around with you.

The instructions and files you need to build your own are all on the Hackaday page, so be sure to check it out. And if this seems too time-consuming for you, why not try these Raspberry Pi projects you can create within an hour?
